summary: Expanded and refactored `tools/split_suggester.py` so it could handle more assembly/data layouts and already-converted data syntax. The tool gained argparse-based CLI flags, a Python 3 shebang, explicit exit-code documentation, a `ProgramError` path, an `if __name__ == "__main__"`-style review-driven structure, support for `.float` and `.double` in `.sdata2` in addition to `.4byte`, preliminary bytecounting for additional sections, console-only/debug modes, map-file parsing as a function, and richer output including literals used per generated C file. `requirements.txt` added `black` and `isort`, likely to support formatting cleanup. The PR mattered because `split_suggester.py` became useful on files after `convert_data.py` had already run, letting contributors verify split suggestions against converted literals rather than only raw `.4byte` data.

key_files.role: Main tooling update: widened section/literal parsing, refactored argument parsing, reorganized execution flow, and improved split suggestion output.

what_changed: Changed `split_suggester.py` usage from positional arguments to `argparse` flags such as `-s/--asmFile`, `-m/--mapFile`, `-d/--debug`, `-c/--consoleOnly`, and `-v/--version`.
Added a Python 3 shebang and removed old Python 2/3 compatibility branching around `input()`/`raw_input()`.
Documented exit codes in the file header: success, invalid CLI input, missing `.sdata2`, and failure to parse floats data.
Introduced `Version = "1.2.1"` and a `ProgramError(RuntimeError)` class carrying an `error_code`.
Moved map parsing into `parseMapFile(mapFilePath)`, returning a dictionary keyed by function start address.
Moved assembly parsing into `parseAssemblyFile(args)` with local tracking state for file sections, functions, float data, float types, suspicious padding, and uncertain bytecounts.
Reworked section handling from hardcoded `.text`, `.data`, and `.sdata2` booleans into a `fileSections` ordered dictionary keyed by encountered `.section` names.
Added support for `.float` and `.double` literal syntax in `.sdata2`, not just `.4byte`, including re-encoding decoded literals to inspect f32/f64 byte patterns.
Renamed float type labels in the parsing logic from older wording like `single`/`double` to `f32`/`f64` in the diff excerpt.
Added detection and warning cases where `convert_data.py` appears to have described a literal as `.float` when the usage suggests f64, or `.double` when the usage suggests f32.
Tracked all labels referenced by a function while also maintaining `uniqueLabels` through `Function.addLabel()`.
Expanded output to show total functions, total floats in `.sdata2`, per-section byte totals, uncertain bytecount warnings, optional debug dumps, and map-derived names.
PR body states the tool now prints all literals used in each generated C output file at the top, commented out by default.
Added preliminary support for parsing other file sections such as rodata, bss, sbss, etc., though exact completeness is only evidenced by the PR body and partial diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Made the splitter compatible with assembly after `convert_data.py` conversion by accepting `.float` and `.double`, allowing the tool to validate converted data instead of requiring raw `.4byte` input.
Inferred literal type from actual load instructions (`lfs` implies f32, `lfd` implies f64) and cross-checked that against data directives, which is a useful decomp sanity check.
Preserved raw-byte reasoning even when the assembly directive already contains decoded float text by packing floats/doubles back into big-endian bytes before interpreting them.
Kept duplicate label reference order in `labels` while adding `uniqueLabels`, preserving both call/reference frequency information and deduplicated summaries.
Generalized section bytecounting with an ordered section map instead of fixed `.data`/`.sdata2` counters, making later section support easier.
Added a console-only mode so contributors can inspect split suggestions without generating template files.
Turned command-line parsing into a real `argparse` interface after reviewer feedback, improving usability and future extensibility.
Separated parsing functions from top-level execution, making the script easier to test, import, and maintain.
decomp_lessons: Decomp tooling should handle both raw assembler data (`.4byte`) and post-conversion literal syntax (`.float`, `.double`) because contributors may run tools in different orders.
For float matching, infer intended data width from instruction usage (`lfs` versus `lfd`) rather than trusting only the data directive emitted by a conversion tool.
When decoded literals obscure original bytes, re-pack the numeric value into target-endian bytes before checking whether it behaves as f32 or f64.
Split-suggestion tools are most useful when they report enough context for manual judgment: function counts, section byte totals, map names, suspicious padding, and per-file literals.
Avoid hardcoding only the known sections of today; decomp assembly often contains rodata/bss/sbss or other sections that affect file boundaries and byte totals.
Keep CLI tools import-safe by putting main execution under `if __name__ == "__main__"` and isolating parsing in functions.
Use explicit exit codes for contributor-facing tooling so failures can be distinguished in scripts or CI-like workflows.
When byte totals depend on ambiguous integer directives, report uncertainty rather than pretending exactness.
naming_conventions: CLI argument names introduced in the diff use camelCase-style long options matching existing script variable names: `--asmFile`, `--mapFile`, `--consoleOnly`.
Float type names in the new logic use C-style width terminology `f32` and `f64`, replacing less precise `single`/`double` labels in the shown code.
Map names are only accepted when they do not start with `zz` and do not end with `_`, matching the existing heuristic shown in `Function.checkForMapName()`.
The new helper `grammarfyList` is named to describe producing a human-readable English list, used in uncertain-bytecount output.
The script version variable is capitalized as `Version`, as shown in the diff, rather than typical all-caps `VERSION`; no review comment on this naming is present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Parse `.section` directives to track the active section and accumulate byte totals per section.
Strip `//` and `#` comments before interpreting assembly lines.
Use `.global` as a signal that a function boundary is certain.
Treat lines starting with `/*` as code lines and count them as 4 bytes in `.text`.
Treat `blr` as a likely function end, but discredit that end if a later label is the target of a branch from inside the current function.
Identify sdata labels referenced from code by looking for `lbl_` in instruction lines and extracting the label before any `@` suffix.
Classify float labels from load instructions: `lfs` records an f32 use and `lfd` records an f64 use.
For `.sdata2`, process `.4byte`, `.float`, and `.double` directives and use big-endian `struct.pack`/`struct.unpack` to reconstruct f32/f64 values.
For possible doubles represented as two 4-byte values, combine the first and second words before decoding as f64.
Track suspicious padding when f32 data is followed in a way that may indicate a file boundary.
Count `.4byte` and `.float` as 4 bytes for non-special sections, and `.asciz` by string length plus null aligned to 4 bytes, per the diff excerpt.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied `Done.` and the diff shows `import argparse` plus `parseArguments()`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Requested moving main execution into an `if __name__ == "__main__"` block.
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied `Done.`; exact final block is not visible in the excerpt, but the review thread indicates it was addressed.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Requested using multiline format string literals for long print blocks.
review_feedback.resolution: Author replied `Done.`; the excerpt shows print formatting was being revised, but the complete final formatting is not fully visible.

review_feedback.author: DRGN-DRC
review_feedback.feedback: Noted in the PR discussion that the tool was still useful as-is and that they personally used it whenever starting work on a file.
review_feedback.resolution: Merged, with a stated plan by the author to come back later and clean it up more.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `tools/split_suggester.py` for the cleanup DRGN-DRC said they planned after PR #755.
Search for uses or documentation of `split_suggester.py -s` and `--consoleOnly` to see whether contributors adopted the new CLI.
Look for bugfixes around `.float`/`.double` parsing after this PR, especially cases involving `convert_data.py` output.
Inspect later requirements/tooling PRs to confirm why `black` and `isort` were added and whether formatting was standardized project-wide.
Search review history for section-bytecount or `.bss`/`.sbss` follow-ups, since this PR only claims preliminary support for other sections.
